page: Amazon Web Services r8id.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The instance provides 4 vCPUs allocated from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. Local storage is bundled as a 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r8id.xlarge demonstrates strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2156.0 and top-tier text processing capabilities. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks are highly efficient, with gzip compression reaching 34.41 GB/sec. However, multi-core scaling presents a performance tradeoff; both stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression fall into the lower 25th percentile. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is average, measuring 39.12 GB/sec for reads and 29.24 GB/sec for writes, whereas cached small-block reads are relatively weak.

Given its high memory density and integrated NVMe SSD, the r8id.xlarge offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive multi-core parallelization. The inclusion of Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instructions supports specialized vector and matrix operations, as reflected in average performance for small-model LLM inference. This server is best suited for database operations, caching layers like Redis, text processing pipelines, and memory-intensive web serving applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r8id.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs across 2 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a 237 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server delivers strong single-core performance, particularly in text processing and single-threaded compression benchmarks. However, multi-core scaling is a notable tradeoff, with multi-threaded benchmarks performing in the lower quartile. The instance includes advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512, making it highly capable for memory-intensive tasks. The r8id.xlarge is well-suited for database operations, Redis caching, and text processing workloads where memory density and local storage speed are prioritized over high multi-core compute density.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 237 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for databases and text processing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high-speed DDR5 memory, local NVMe storage, and strong single-core processing capabilities for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB DDR5 RAM
Bundled 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store
Strong single-core performance in text processing and compression
Tradeoff in multi-core scaling and cached memory bandwidth
Suited for database operations, Redis caching, and text processing
